title: Asset Management Market Surges to $18,071 Billion by 2035 as AI, IoT, and Predictive Maintenance Transform Enterprise Operations | Research by SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global Asset Management Market is projected to grow from $885.92 billion in 2025 to $18,071 billion by 2035, with a CAGR of 35.2%. Growth is driven by AI and IoT integration enabling predictive maintenance, with software dominating at 64% market share and cloud-based deployment leading. North America holds 39% market share, while the U.S. market is expected to grow from $96.69 billion to $677.89 billion by 2035.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: IBM Maximo platform is highlighted as a leading enterprise asset management solution managing physical equipment across manufacturing, energy, and infrastructure sectors, positioning the company to benefit from the 35.2% CAGR market growth.
Oracle EAM platform is mentioned as a major player in enterprise asset management, well-positioned to capture growth from the expanding market driven by AI and IoT adoption.

SAP PM platform is identified as a key enterprise asset management solution, positioned to benefit from the market's strong growth trajectory and increasing demand for predictive maintenance capabilities.
Listed among key players in the asset management market, positioned to benefit from cloud-based deployment dominance and digital transformation trends driving market expansion.
Identified as a key player in asset management solutions, well-positioned to capitalize on growth in manufacturing and industrial sectors driving the market's 35.2% CAGR.

Recent 2025 launch of ABB Ability SmartMaster for predictive maintenance demonstrates active innovation and market engagement, positioning the company to benefit from the growing adoption of AI-driven asset management solutions.

title: Mobile Forensics Market Surges to $9.99 billion at a CAGR 11.8% by 2031 | Exclusive Report by MarketsandMarkets™
author: Marketsandmarkets™
description: The global mobile forensics market is projected to grow from USD 5.72 billion in 2026 to USD 9.99 billion by 2031, driven by increasing complexity of mobile ecosystems, encrypted applications, and privacy-focused device architectures. North America leads with 43.1% market share, while law enforcement represents the largest vertical. Key growth drivers include AI-driven automation, convergence of mobile and cloud forensics platforms, and rising demand for forensic workstations.

title: Softbank's Masayoshi Son Isn't Worried About An AI Bubble— He's Hunting For The Next Trillion-Dollar Industry: 'Just The Beginning'
author: Namrata Sen
description: SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son believes the AI revolution is '50x bigger' than the dot-com boom and will last 50-100 years, dismissing concerns of an AI bubble. Son has identified humanoid and industrial robotics with 'physical AI' as the next trillion-dollar opportunity, evidenced by SoftBank's $5.4 billion acquisition of ABB's robotics division in October and plans for a $100 billion IPO of its Roze AI venture.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: CEO's bullish outlook on AI and robotics as trillion-dollar opportunities, strategic $5.4B acquisition of ABB's robotics division, and planned $100B IPO for Roze AI demonstrate aggressive expansion and confidence in future growth prospects.
While the $5.4B sale of its robotics division to SoftBank represents a significant transaction, the article does not provide information about ABB's overall strategic direction or market outlook.

Meta's acquisition of humanoid robotics startup Assured Robot Intelligence and integration into its Superintelligence Labs demonstrates commitment to the robotics and AI sector, aligning with industry growth trends.

title: Battery Energy Storage System Market: $198.8 Bn by 2031, Fueled by On-Grid Utility Storage and North America, Europe Investments | Says Mordor Intelligence
author: Mordor Intelligence
description: The global battery energy storage system (BESS) market is projected to grow from $89.89 billion in 2026 to $198.88 billion by 2031 at a CAGR of 17.21%. Growth is driven by falling lithium-ion battery costs, utility-scale procurement mandates, grid modernization investments, and supportive government policies like the U.S. Inflation Reduction Act and EU's Net-Zero Industry Act. Asia-Pacific leads the market, while the Middle East and Africa show the fastest growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a key BESS company with exposure to utility-scale storage deployments and grid-forming inverter technologies driving new value streams.
Specialized BESS company positioned to benefit from grid-forming inverter requirements and utility-scale storage deployments across regions.
Listed as a major BESS company with exposure to power management and grid infrastructure modernization investments.
Energy management and storage solutions provider positioned to benefit from solar-plus-storage projects achieving cost competitiveness globally.
Major battery manufacturer benefiting from utility-scale storage mandates and supply chain localization efforts in North America and Europe.
Listed as a major BESS component provider with exposure to power conversion systems and grid modernization investments.
Major energy infrastructure company benefiting from grid modernization and utility-scale storage deployment trends globally.

title: Smart Ports Global Strategic Business Report 2026: Market to Reach $7.3 Billion by 2032 from $1.6 Billion in 2025 - Technological Innovations in IoT, AI, and Blockchain Strengthen Business Case
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global smart ports market is projected to grow from $1.6 billion in 2025 to $7.3 billion by 2032, with a CAGR of 23.6%. Growth is driven by increasing global trade, digitalization, and adoption of IoT, AI, and blockchain technologies for port automation, cargo management, and sustainability. Key regions include the U.S. ($482.5M in 2025) and China (expected to reach $1.2B by 2032).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Featured as a key player in smart ports market, well-positioned to capitalize on digital transformation and technology integration services in port operations.
Included among major players in the smart ports market, with opportunities in automation, energy management systems, and IoT solutions for port operations.
Featured as a key player in smart ports, with strong positioning in AI, blockchain, cloud computing, and data analytics solutions driving port digitization.
Listed as a company featured in the smart ports market report, positioned to benefit from growing demand for digital transformation and technology services in maritime logistics.

Featured as a key player in the growing smart ports market, which is experiencing strong growth at 23.6% CAGR. ABB's involvement in automation and digital technologies positions it well to benefit from increased port digitization demand.
